more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Over 200 young people receive vocational training and equipment under the YTCDT programme
more.description: Source: Over 200 young people receive vocational training and equipment under the YTCDT programme – herald Muchaneta Chimuka OVER 200 youths from Domboshova under headman Parirehwa, have received an assortment of equipment from Youth Together for Community Development and Transformation (YTCDT) that will enable them to engage in income-generating projects. more.title: First Capital finalises US$30m Kingdom Hotel disposal to ASB
more.description: Source: First Capital finalises US$30m Kingdom Hotel disposal to ASB – herald Tapiwanashe Mangwiro First Capital Bank Limited has completed the long-awaited disposal of Makasa Sun, drawing the curtain on a US$30 million transaction and handing control of the iconic Kingdom Hotel in Victoria Falls to United Arab Emirates-based ASB Hospitality. more.title: BAT Zimbabwe implements defensive strategy to protect market share
more.description: Source: BAT Zimbabwe implements defensive strategy to protect market share – herald Business Reporter BAT Zimbabwe is implementing a defensive strategy anchored in product portfolio optimisation and operational discipline to protect its market position against growing competition. more.description: Source: Why Zimbabwean firms must abandon old pricing playbooks – herald Nelson Gahadza Senior Business Reporter COMPANIES in Zimbabwe have been urged to rethink their pricing models and adopt more innovative strategies to remain competitive in a price-sensitive market increasingly exposed to regional competition. more.title: New ZiG notes boost daily transactions
more.description: Source: New ZiG notes boost daily transactions – herald Herald Reporter NEW Zimbabwe Gold banknotes are easing cash shortages and improving daily transactions, with economists saying long-term confidence will depend on the currency’s ability to retain value. The ZiG notes, introduced in phases last month, are now circulating widely through automated teller machines (ATMs) and [...] The post New ZiG notes boost daily transactions appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Zimbabwe exports US$11,7 million worth of goods to Europe in a month
more.description: Source: Zimbabwe exports US$11,7 million worth of goods to Europe in a month – NewZimbabwe.com ZIMBABWE exported goods worth US$11,7 million to the European Union (EU) in the month of March 2026 in a development which further demystifies the existence of economic sanctions against the African nation. more.title: Something is brewing in Zimbabwe
more.description: Source: Something is brewing in Zimbabwe – Bulawayo24 News An unannounced visit by South African President Cyril Ramaphosa to Zimbabwe has sparked political speculation after he was hosted by President Emmerson Mnangagwa at his private Precabe Farm in Kwekwe, where several influential business figures were also present. more.title: The “Crash Game” Phenomenon and Why They are Redefining Mobile-First Fintech in Africa
more.description: Traditional banking across Africa isn’t being disrupted loudly, but the shift is unmistakable. Simple, interactive mobile experiences are changing how people move money day to day. What stands out is how entertainment has become a gateway, pulling more users into digital payments and, in turn, expanding financial inclusion.
